attempt: Transfer the TICKET68 lesson: a coarse cyclic quotient is not decisive if a theorem-plausible refinement turns it into a DAG. The problem-specific task is to prove refinement completeness or expose a persistent refined cycle.

bounded_result.candidate_theorem: Every exact-gap parity leakage cycle is either broken by a refined retained-mass DAG, or a compatible exact-gap cycle persists through the sieve refinement.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: a compatible exact-gap branch that remains cyclic after parity and retained-mass refinement
bounded_result.frontier_rule: A finite cyclic quotient is not a proof obstruction by itself. It must survive theorem-plausible refinement and be compatible with an infinite object.

obstruction: This is a transferred theorem target, not a solution.
candidate_theorem: Every exact-gap parity leakage cycle is either broken by a refined retained-mass DAG, or a compatible exact-gap cycle persists through the sieve refinement.
next_experiment: Build the problem-specific refined frontier and test whether cycles persist after the added coordinates.
claim_boundary: No twin prime proof and no certified twin prime counterexample.
